model_output_raw_text: {"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"Increase model capacity in the compact CNN and add mild label smoothing. The current run improved with optimizer/schedule tuning, so the next low-risk gain is to give the model more representational power while slightly regularizing the loss.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 2","new":"DEPTH = 3","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","new":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"The current 3-block model is improving, but the loss is still high enough that extra capacity is likely the next useful gain. Widening the backbone and classifier is the simplest way to add representation power while keeping the existing schedule and regularization intact.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"The wider model is now learning well, so the next likely gain is a slightly more aggressive OneCycle peak LR with a bit less decoupled decay. This keeps the training loop unchanged while letting the current architecture fit faster under the fixed step budget.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"The current model is still improving, which suggests the remaining headroom is more likely architectural than optimizer-side. Adding one more convolutional stage and a slightly larger classifier is the simplest way to increase feature hierarchy without changing the training loop.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 3","new":"DEPTH = 4","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"The 4-stage model is still gaining from extra representation power, which suggests the remaining headroom is capacity-limited rather than schedule-limited. Widening the stem is the cleanest next change and preserves the tuned optimizer and schedule.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 32","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"Replace the dense flatten head with global average pooling so the widened backbone feeds a more regularized classifier. This keeps the tuned optimizer and schedule intact while fixing the previous application error by editing the existing class body directly.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        self.features = nn.Sequential(*layers)
        feat_size = IMAGE_SIZE // (2**DEPTH)
        feat_dim = in_ch * feat_size * feat_size
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(feat_dim, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )","new":"        self.features = nn.Sequential(*layers)
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d(1)
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(in_ch, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        return self.classifier(self.features(x))","new":"        return self.classifier(self.pool(self.features(x)))","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"After the global-average-pooling refactor, the classifier head is the main bottleneck. Widening that head is a low-risk way to recover capacity while keeping the better regularization and training schedule in place.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"The 256-unit head was too wide and hurt validation loss. A 192-unit head keeps the pooled backbone expressive while backing off from the overfit regime introduced by the last change.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 192","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["layout"],"rationale":"The 192-unit head was worse than the earlier 128-unit setting, so the next move is to restore that narrower head and ease the decoupled decay slightly. With GAP already in place, the model likely needs less constraint rather than more capacity.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 192","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"The lower decay did not help, so restore the stronger regularization that already produced the best visible loss and soften the peak learning rate a bit for finer convergence. This keeps the same architecture and schedule shape while retuning the optimization scale around the current sweet spot.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"The reduced peak LR was clearly too conservative for this setup. Move slightly above the best-known 1.5e-3 setting to see if the OneCycle schedule can push a bit harder without losing stability.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.6e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"The last higher-LR probe regressed, so the safest next move is to return to the previously better 1.5e-3 peak learning rate. That restores the known-good scale without changing the rest of the tuned configuration.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.6e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["layout"],"rationale":"The current configuration is back at its best visible loss, so the next step is to probe a very small regularization increase rather than reshaping the model again. A tiny dropout rate is the least intrusive way to test whether the GAP backbone still has a bit of overfit headroom left.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.02","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The dropout probe clearly over-regularized the model. Revert that change and trim label smoothing a bit so the already-regularized GAP backbone can fit the CIFAR-10 boundary more tightly.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.02","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","new":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.03)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"summaries","declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"Restore the best-performing label smoothing and test a slightly longer OneCycle warmup. The earlier losses suggest the model prefers the original regularization level, and the peak LR may benefit from a gentler ramp.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.03)","new":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"pct_start=0.15,","new":"pct_start=0.2,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"summaries","declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"Restore the proven 0.15 warmup and make the OneCycle tail less aggressive. The 0.2 warmup regressed, and with only 256 steps the schedule may benefit from keeping a higher learning rate near the end instead of cooling too fast.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"pct_start=0.2,","new":"pct_start=0.15,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"final_div_factor=1000.0,","new":"final_div_factor=200.0,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"summaries","declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The shorter tail was slightly worse than the original schedule. Restore the longer OneCycle decay so the last part of the short budget can cool more gently, which was the better-known setting.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"final_div_factor=200.0,","new":"final_div_factor=1000.0,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The model is back at the best visible configuration, so the remaining room is likely in very small regularization tuning. Reduce label smoothing slightly to see whether the pooled backbone prefers a bit less target softness than 0.05.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","new":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.04)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}
